research.title: The Many Senses of Visual Similarity: A Text-Prompted Image Perceptual Metric
research.summary: Sheng-Yu Wang, Yotam Nitzan, Aaron Hertzmann et al. — Human visual similarity judgments are context-dependent. Existing perceptual similarity metrics, however, collapse these nuances into a single…

research.title: Patch Policy: Efficient Embodied Control via Dense Visual Representations
research.summary: Gaoyue Zhou, Zichen Jeff Cui, Ada Langford et al. — Pretrained dense visual features from Vision Transformers (ViTs) are powerful yet have been underutilized in robot learning. Modern robot policies either compress each observation into a single global token, or rely on…

research.title: Automated Discovery Has No Universally Superior Harness
research.summary: Akshat Gupta, Jermaine Lei, Alexander Lu et al. — Autonomous discovery systems such as OpenEvolve and TTT-Discover are often used as general-purpose harnesses. However, in practice these are composite systems combining several design choices about archives, parent…

research.title: It's Not What You Say, It's How You Say It: Evaluating LLM Responses to Expressions of Belief
research.summary: Kevin Du, Clara Kümpel, Michelle Wastl et al. — Users frequently express their beliefs to large language models (LLMs). In some situations, the LLM should accept these contextual beliefs as true.

research.title: Simple Domain Generalization for Strong Pixel-Level Image Tampering Detection in Modern VLMs
research.summary: Yi Tang, Xinyi Shang, Jiacheng Cui et al. — Modern vision-language models (VLMs) have significantly improved image generation and editing capabilities, making pixel-level image tampering detection increasingly important yet challenging under cross-model and…

research.title: Logical Judgments Under Pressure: Diagnosing Syllogistic Stability with Learned Soft Prefixes
research.summary: Brian K Chen — To test how correct logical judgments respond to learned context, we prepend a soft prefix to an exactly labeled syllogistic reasoning benchmark while keeping the model fixed. Soft prefixes are opaque continuous…

research.title: Causal Discovery on Irregular Time Series
research.summary: Martim Penim, Ricardo Ribeiro Pereira, Jacopo Bono et al. — Causal discovery methods have shown strong performance in temporal systems, but they typically rely on regular and discrete lag structures, limiting their applicability to regularly sampled data.

research.title: Vector Search As Nearest Neighbor Matching: RAG-based Policy Learning in Causal Inference
research.summary: Masahiro Kato, Taka Kato — We propose one-step and two-step methods for policy learning with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). We formulate RAG-based action selection under the potential outcome framework.

research.summary: Naoto Usuyama, Jeya Maria Jose Valanarasu, Sicong Yao et al. — Foundation models have emerged as a driving force in computational pathology, with the potential to transform cancer diagnosis, prognosis, and treatment selection by learning transferable representations from…

research.title: SWE-Pruner Pro: The Coder LLM Already Knows What to Prune
research.summary: Yuhang Wang, Yuling Shi, Shaoqiu Zhang et al. — Pruning long context for coding agents has been a vital technology for efficient context management. While existing context pruning methods such as SWE-Pruner realize this by attaching a separate code classifier, we…

research.title: Unveiling Invariant and Transferable Latent Factors Across Heterogeneous Environments via ATLAS
research.summary: Yihong Gu, Katherine Liao, Tianxi Cai — This paper considers a multi-environment factor model in which high-dimensional covariates are collected from heterogeneous environments, with auxiliary labels available in a subset of these environments.

research.title: Learning Adaptive Safety Margins for Visual Navigation
research.summary: Junyi Hu, Shuaihang Yuan, Geeta Chandra Raju Bethala et al. — Robots in cluttered indoor spaces often fail not because they cannot generate collision-free paths, but because a fixed safety margin is mis-calibrated: conservative margins cause detours and timeouts, while permissive…

labs.title: Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models
labs.summary: OpenAI shares lessons from deploying long-running AI models, highlighting new safety risks, observed failures, and improved safeguards through iterative deployment.

labs.title: A scorecard for the AI age
labs.summary: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

labs.title: Why teens deserve access to safe AI
labs.summary: Learn how OpenAI is making ChatGPT safer for teens with age-appropriate protections, learning tools, parental controls, and expert partnerships.

labs.title: Our approach to bioresilience
labs.summary: The global biosecurity landscape is rapidly evolving. Today, Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs are sharing our joint approach to bioresilience . To support government bodies and non-profit organizations during novel outbreaks, Isomorphic Labs has established a focused unit to rapidly deploy its drug design engine to design medical countermeasures that could address both naturally occurring pandemics and potential…

labs.title: How Cars24 scales conversations and builds faster with OpenAI
labs.summary: Cars24 uses OpenAI-powered voice and chat agents to handle 1M+ monthly conversation minutes, recover 12% of lost leads, and bring agentic workflows to teams across the company.

labs.title: Towards demystifying the creativity of diffusion models
labs.summary: Zhengdao Chen, Research Scientist, Google Research We show that a diffusion model’s creativity (its ability to generate novel data, rather than just memorize its training set) is a mathematical consequence of neural networks learning a "smoothed" version of the score function, driving the model to interpolate between training data points along the hidden data manifold. In a diffusion model, the moving particles are…

labs.title: The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
labs.summary: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

labs.title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
labs.summary: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.

labs.title: How to manage AI investments in the agentic era
labs.summary: Learn how enterprises can manage AI investments in the agentic era by measuring useful work per dollar, improving efficiency, and scaling high-value workflows.

labs.title: How sales teams use ChatGPT Work
labs.summary: See how sales teams can use ChatGPT Work to create pipeline briefs, meeting prep packets, forecast reviews, account plans, and stalled-deal diagnoses from real work inputs.

labs.title: How data science teams use ChatGPT Work
labs.summary: See how data science teams can use ChatGPT Work to build root-cause briefs, impact readouts, KPI memos, scoped analyses, and dashboard specs from real work inputs.

labs.title: Empowering India’s next generation of innovators with ATL Saathi
labs.summary: Atal Tinkering Labs (ATL) is bringing access to new technology—such as 3D printing, IoT, and robotics—for over 1.1 crore students across India. The application provides summarized modules, AI-generated infographics, video overviews, and interactive quizzes for 12 core modules from the ATL prescribed curriculum. Powering the ATL Saathi, the Gemini model provides the underlying intelligence to transform tinkering labs…

startups.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
startups.summary: A federal judge gave final approval Monday of Anthropic’s landmark $1.5 billion settlement of a class action copyright lawsuit, Reuters reported . District Court for the Northern District of California issued a preliminary approval of the settlement last year, after ruling that Anthropic had illegally downloaded and stored millions of copyrighted books. Alsup sided with Anthropic on the core issue.

startups.title: Colossal Biosciences reportedly in talks to raise new capital at $20B–$30B valuation
startups.summary: Colossal co-founder and CEO Ben Lamm told TechCrunch last year that the company sees three revenue streams for the business. Lamm told TechCrunch last year that the company also plans to spin off its artificial animal womb technology, which could have applications for human fertility treatment. Marina Temkin is a venture capital and startups reporter at TechCrunch.

startups.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
startups.summary: Chris Fall, the director of the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI), has resigned, the agency confirmed to multiple news outlets. Commerce Department invoked an obscure export control directive that effectively forced Anthropic to pull its Mythos and Fable models from the market. The administration was weighing efforts to somehow ban Chinese open models, Axios reported .

startups.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
startups.summary: Alphabet, Google’s parent company, is designing a new server chip to help its in-house Gemini models operate more efficiently. In June, OpenAI announced its first custom chip, an inference processor dubbed Jalapeño . News of the more efficient Frozen v2 chip appears to have assuaged investors, giving Google a boost ahead of its earnings report later this week.

startups.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
startups.summary: The server replies with its own capabilities and hands back a session ID… From then on, the client sends that session ID on every request so the server knows it’s the same conversation. Picture a real deployment. That’s been a significant headache for anyone running an MCP server at scale, and part of the reason we haven’t seen more companies ship large-scale, first-party MCP integrations despite all the hype around…

startups.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
startups.summary: Nearly a year ago, Elon Musk-owned X announced it would begin rebuilding the Android version of its app, which had not held up well compared with its iOS counterpart. We've completely rebuilt the Android X app from the ground up. Existing Android users can get the new X app by updating their existing app through the Google Play Store.

startups.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
startups.summary: Concerns over Chinese models come in several flavors. Advocates for open AI say that the frontier companies are creating a false binary between innovation and closed models. Some US companies, including Thinking Machines Lab and Nvidia, are trying to make a business around releasing open models.

startups.title: Natural raises $30M to reinvent payments for AI agents — and take on Stripe
startups.summary: But these financial rails were built for human-initiated transactions, not autonomous AI agents. By integrating Natural’s infrastructure, companies can allow their agents to make autonomous payments, collect funds, and transact with both humans and other agents. Green was attracted by Natural’s broader ambitions.

startups.title: Judge pauses $110B Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
startups.summary: Paramount Skydance’s proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) has hit a roadblock after a judge temporarily paused the deal in response to a lawsuit filed by a coalition of 12 state attorneys general who argue that the merger would harm competition. Aisha is a consumer news reporter at TechCrunch.

startups.title: Adobe camera app’s new feature will critique your photos using AI
startups.summary: Adobe is adding new AI-powered features to its experimental iOS camera app called Project Indigo, launched last year . The app previously offered pro controls, multi-frame super-resolution, and different capture modes, and is now adding features that will use LLMs (large language models) to critique photos and provide editing suggestions. Photography apps, including Apple Photos, Google Photos, and Adobe Photoshop,…

startups.title: Hackers are exploiting recently patched WordPress bugs, putting millions of websites at risk
startups.summary: Hackers are breaking into websites that run vulnerable versions of the popular blogging software WordPress, according to several cybersecurity firms. One estimate puts the number of vulnerable WordPress websites at tens of millions as of Monday. The vulnerable versions of WordPress are 6.9.0 through 6.9.4, and 7.0.0 to 7.0.1.

startups.title: YouTube clarifies policies around AI slop and upsetting videos
startups.summary: To discourage creators from using AI and other tools to make low-quality content, YouTube’s policy now explains that there are three types of videos under the broader category of “inauthentic content” that cannot be monetized. YouTube already had policies around AI content. The second category focuses on what YouTube calls “off-putting” content, or content designed to be distressing or emotionally manipulative to…

wider_tech.title: Five US tech giants' hidden debts soar to $1.65T on opaque AI funding
wider_tech.summary: Data center leases, GPU supply contracts raise liabilities at Meta, Oracle, Nikkei study shows A Meta data center in the state of Georgia. The company's off-balance-sheet debt is about $420 billion, nearly triple its transparent debt. © AP PALO ALTO, California -- Hidden debt at U.S. tech giants swelled eightfold in roughly four years to an estimated $1.65 trillion as artificial intelligence investments ballooned, a… (279 points, 153 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: Flock Credibility Lost as It Repeatedly Lies to City Councils, Police, & Public
wider_tech.summary: At the end of that meeting, the Oshkosh City Council approved a contract with Flock. The very next morning, the city learned that Flock had lied . The CEO admitted Flock was used for immigration enforcement but argued that such matters were not Flock’s problem . (402 points, 121 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
wider_tech.summary: Songs from Beyoncé, Britney Spears, Johnny Cash, Harry Styles, and many more are on the list. Songs from Beyoncé, Britney Spears, Johnny Cash, Harry Styles, and many more are on the list. In 2024, Sony, along with Universal Music Group and Warner Records, filed a lawsuit against Udio and the AI music-maker Suno.

wider_tech.title: The FCC is planning to retroactively ban disguised DJI gadgets
wider_tech.summary: The FCC’s currently taking public comments for 30 days first. All save XAG were brought to the FCC’s attention by Konrad Iturbe, who figured out how to track the “FCC front companies” last year. The FCC also cites our story about Xtra .

wider_tech.title: The Odyssey turned me into an IMAX believer
wider_tech.summary: ﻿Seeing the film’s most impressive monsters in a larger format is a completely different experience. ﻿Seeing the film’s most impressive monsters in a larger format is a completely different experience. After seeing Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey for the first time early last week, I came away impressed, but somewhat conflicted about two of the film’s more fantastical set pieces. It also helps that the movie is…

wider_tech.title: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
wider_tech.summary: Index funds are supposed to be the safest way to invest — does SpaceX change that? But SpaceX isn’t a reason to avoid index funds, he says. Index rebalancing funds made out like bandits .

wider_tech.title: Get Borderlands 3, Risk of Rain 2 and 13 other great PC games for $15
wider_tech.summary: The aptly-named “2K Megahits 2026 Bundle” from Humble includes 15 Steam games for $15. One of my personal favorite games, Risk of Rain 2 , is included in the bundle. The bundle also notably includes three games from the Borderlands universe.

wider_tech.title: The PlayStation replica ornament is an homage to a great, yet fragile console
wider_tech.summary: The $28.99 replica plays the signature boot sound, and reminds me of my first-ever DIY tech repair. The $28.99 replica plays the signature boot sound, and reminds me of my first-ever DIY tech repair. You probably know the signature PlayStation boot sound .

wider_tech.title: Ford’s $30,000 electric truck: all the news about the company’s big EV re-do
wider_tech.summary: The company scrapped plans to build a next-gen electric truck, codenamed T3 , and an electric commercial van. To say that Ford has struggled to make profitable electric vehicles would be an understatement. But Ford still sees EVs as the future — just not oversize ones with “no path to profitability,” like the Lightning, as Andrew Frick, president of Ford Model e and Ford Blue, said last year.

wider_tech.title: Human mathematicians are being outcounterexampled
wider_tech.summary: So of course my first question was “is the counterexample formalized in Lean”. Interesting data point. Another counterexample! (342 points, 138 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: Nativ: Run frontier open models locally on your Mac
wider_tech.summary: Download and run open models on Apple Silicon — no accounts, no subscriptions, no cloud. Run standout open models from Google, Cohere, and Liquid AI. We built in the open. (274 points, 87 comments on Hacker News)
